title: Senior police officers
description: Determine and formulate policies and provide overall direction of companies or private and public sector organizations within guidelines set up by a board of directors or similar governing body. Plan, direct, or coordinate operational activities at the highest level of management with the help of subordinate executives and staff managers. Communicate and coordinate with management, shareholders, customers, and employees to address sustainability issues. Enact or oversee a corporate sustainability strategy. Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. (Based on 20 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze and process evidence at crime scenes, during autopsies, or in the laboratory, wearing protective equipment and using powders and chemicals.
Analyze intelligence data to identify patterns and trends in criminal activity and prepare comprehensive written reports based on research and analysis.
Organize scene search, assigning specific tasks and areas of search to officers while ensuring evidence preservation and documentation of the scene.
Conduct presentations of analytic findings and testify in court, presenting evidence and providing insights from investigations.
Interview survivors, witnesses, and suspects to obtain facts, statements, and additional insights about incidents or criminal activities.
Evaluate records of communications to identify criminal group activities and validate intelligence with data from other sources to establish connections.
Maintain surveillance of establishments to collect identifying information on suspects and gather intelligence through field observations.
Supervise and coordinate the investigation of criminal cases, offering guidance to investigators and ensuring investigations comply with legal standards.
Develop, implement, and review departmental policies, ensuring adherence to legal requirements and effective operational procedures.
Plan, direct, and coordinate operational activities across multiple departments, ensuring resource utilization and compliance with management directives.
Conduct risk assessments related to sustainability and develop plans that outline procedures for responses to emergencies or crises.
Direct or coordinate an organization's financial or budget activities to fund operations, maximize investments, or increase efficiency.
Direct, plan, or implement policies, objectives, or activities of organizations or businesses to ensure continuing operations and increase productivity.
Analyze operations to evaluate performance in meeting objectives and to identify areas for cost reduction or improvement.
Confer with board members, officials, or staff to discuss issues, coordinate activities, or resolve problems.
Explain policies, rules, regulations, or laws to officials and individuals involved in organizational operations.
Plan or direct activities that require coordination with other department managers.
Review financial statements or performance data to measure productivity or identify areas needing improvement.
Develop relationships with environmental regulatory agencies to analyze potential impacts of proposed regulations.
Monitor compliance systems and investigate compliance issues related to operational or safety procedures.
Train staff in regulatory policies and procedures to ensure compliance within the organization.
Prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress, ensuring timely and accurate documentation.
Direct facility maintenance or repair to ensure safe and operational environments.
Supervise fitness or wellness specialists, such as fitness instructors, nutritionists, or health educators.
Develop or coordinate fitness and wellness programs or services.
Serve as a confidential point of contact for employees to communicate with management about issues or report irregularities.
Maintain documentation of compliance activities, such as complaints received or investigation outcomes.
Consult with corporate attorneys as necessary to address complex legal compliance issues.
Collaborate with HR departments to ensure consistent disciplinary actions for compliance standard violations.
Advise management on the implementation or operation of compliance programs.
Provide employee training on compliance-related topics, policies, or procedures.
Report violations of compliance or regulatory standards to the appropriate enforcement agencies as required.
Identify environmental contamination sources and develop strategies to eliminate associated risks.
Prepare and submit permit applications for demolition, cleanup, or construction projects.
Coordinate on-site activities for environmental cleanup or remediation projects to ensure compliance with laws and standards.
Design or implement measures to improve water, air, and soil quality of contaminated sites or military test sites.
Conduct feasibility or cost-benefit studies for environmental remediation projects.
Review or evaluate proposals, designs, or projects to ensure compliance with regulations and standards.
Develop relationships with customers, site managers, or authorities to ensure project objectives are met.
Monitor and maintain records of daily facility operations to ensure efficiency and compliance.
Train or coordinate the training of employees in operational and safety procedures.
Collaborate on developing sustainability strategies for projects to address environmental impacts.
